verses.text: The Spirit brings him to the east gate, where princes give wicked counsel, boasting, 'This city is the caldron, and we be the flesh' — safe within its walls. False security. Men comfort themselves with proud sayings while judgment nears.

The Spirit brings him to the east gate, where princes give wicked counsel, boasting, 'This city is the caldron, and we be the flesh' — safe within its walls. False security. Men comfort themselves with proud sayings while judgment nears.

The Spirit brings him to the east gate, where princes give wicked counsel, boasting, 'This city is the caldron, and we be the flesh' — safe within its walls. False security. Men comfort themselves with proud sayings while judgment nears.
God answers that their slain are the flesh in the caldron, but they themselves shall be brought out to judgment. Their boast is reversed. The very security men trust becomes the proof of their doom.
God answers that their slain are the flesh in the caldron, but they themselves shall be brought out to judgment. Their boast is reversed. The very security men trust becomes the proof of their doom.
God answers that their slain are the flesh in the caldron, but they themselves shall be brought out to judgment. Their boast is reversed. The very security men trust becomes the proof of their doom.
God answers that their slain are the flesh in the caldron, but they themselves shall be brought out to judgment. Their boast is reversed. The very security men trust becomes the proof of their doom.
They feared the sword, and the sword He will bring; they shall fall at the border of Israel and 'know that I am the LORD.' Fear fulfilled. What the guilty dread, God often brings upon them.
They feared the sword, and the sword He will bring; they shall fall at the border of Israel and 'know that I am the LORD.' Fear fulfilled. What the guilty dread, God often brings upon them.
They feared the sword, and the sword He will bring; they shall fall at the border of Israel and 'know that I am the LORD.' Fear fulfilled. What the guilty dread, God often brings upon them.
This city shall not shield them as a caldron; He will judge them for not walking in His statutes. No refuge in disobedience. Walls cannot protect those who have forsaken God's law.
This city shall not shield them as a caldron; He will judge them for not walking in His statutes. No refuge in disobedience. Walls cannot protect those who have forsaken God's law.
As Ezekiel prophesies, Pelatiah suddenly dies, and he falls crying, 'wilt thou make a full end of the remnant?' A startling sign. The prophet's heart breaks for his people even under judgment.
God answers that though scattered far, 'yet will I be to them as a little sanctuary' in the lands of their exile. Mercy in dispersion. Where His people are, God Himself is their refuge and shrine.
God answers that though scattered far, 'yet will I be to them as a little sanctuary' in the lands of their exile. Mercy in dispersion. Where His people are, God Himself is their refuge and shrine.
God answers that though scattered far, 'yet will I be to them as a little sanctuary' in the lands of their exile. Mercy in dispersion. Where His people are, God Himself is their refuge and shrine.
He will gather them, give them the land again, and they shall purge away its detestable idols. Restoration with reform. God's regathering is joined to the cleansing of His people.
He will gather them, give them the land again, and they shall purge away its detestable idols. Restoration with reform. God's regathering is joined to the cleansing of His people.

'I will give them one heart, and... a new spirit... I will take the stony heart out of their flesh, and... give them an heart of flesh.' The gospel promise. God does not merely command obedience; He creates the heart that obeys.

'I will give them one heart, and... a new spirit... I will take the stony heart out of their flesh, and... give them an heart of flesh.' The gospel promise. God does not merely command obedience; He creates the heart that obeys.

But those whose heart still chases idols He will repay; then 'the glory of the LORD went up from... the city,' resting on the eastern mountain. The glory departs. God leaves the temple slowly, mournfully, but He leaves.

But those whose heart still chases idols He will repay; then 'the glory of the LORD went up from... the city,' resting on the eastern mountain. The glory departs. God leaves the temple slowly, mournfully, but He leaves.

But those whose heart still chases idols He will repay; then 'the glory of the LORD went up from... the city,' resting on the eastern mountain. The glory departs. God leaves the temple slowly, mournfully, but He leaves.
The Spirit carries him back in vision to the captives, and he tells them all the LORD has shown. The message is delivered. What God reveals, His servant faithfully reports.
The Spirit carries him back in vision to the captives, and he tells them all the LORD has shown. The message is delivered. What God reveals, His servant faithfully reports.
